creator: adamw@FreeBSD.org
text:
I agree with everything you just said. Running as root IS a bad idea, but breaking existing installs is a bad plan too. I always tend towards giving people the tools and the knowledge and letting them choose whether to shoot themselves in the foot or not.

I think we should:
- Include USERS/GROUPS in the Makefile so the www user is created
- Continue defaulting to root for now but plan to change to www at some point in the future
- Include clear instructions in pkg-message and rc.d/caddy for how to run as www
- Include strongly-worded encouragement to run as www
- I absolutely LOVE the rc(8) portacl script idea! It absolutely should live in base. In the meantime, what about making a port for it and having caddy depend upon it?
